summary: The agent correctly identifies that enamel lamellae are fine longitudinal strips or laminar structures within enamel, resulting from disrupted mineralization during development. Unlike surface elevations or dentinal tubules, these internal structural features reflect developmental anomalies and are distinct from evenly spaced enamel rods.
